title: Tourism
description: UNESCO sites, museums, castles, parks, chocolate, beer experiences, day trips and hidden gems.

items.name: Grand Place, Brussels
items.description: UNESCO World Heritage central square surrounded by ornate 17th-century guildhalls and the Gothic Town Hall.

items.name: Historic Centre of Bruges
items.description: UNESCO-listed medieval city centre of canals and guild houses.
items.ticketInfo: Free to walk; individual sites ticketed

items.name: Belfries of Belgium and France
items.description: A shared UNESCO listing covering belfry towers across Ghent, Bruges, Mechelen and other Belgian and French cities.

items.name: Béguinages of Mechelen
items.description: Mechelen uniquely has two UNESCO World Heritage béguinages (historic communities for religious laywomen).

items.name: Royal Museums of Fine Arts of Belgium
items.description: A group of museums including the Oldmasters Museum (Rubens, Bruegel, Bosch) and the Magritte Museum, the world's largest collection of Magritte's surrealist works (200+ pieces).

items.name: Grand Curtius Museum
items.description: Art, archaeology and decorative arts museum housed in a historic mansion overlooking the Meuse river.

items.name: Gravensteen Castle
items.description: Medieval moated castle built around 1180, the only fully preserved one in Flanders; includes an audio guide covering its history.

items.name: Gaasbeek Castle
items.description: A fairy-tale 16th-century château in the village of Gaasbeek, a short trip from Brussels.

items.name: Citadel of Namur
items.description: One of the largest fortress complexes in Europe, with ~7km of underground tunnels and a cable car link to the city centre.

items.name: Parc du Cinquantenaire
items.description: A grand park in the EU Quarter with a triumphal arch, museums and frequent summer festivals and concerts.

items.name: Bois de la Cambre
items.description: A large landscaped park designed in 1861 at the edge of the Sonian Forest, with a lake, boating, and the Le Chalet Robinson café on its island.

items.name: Pierre Marcolini
items.description: Renowned Brussels chocolatier on Place du Grand Sablon, sourcing cocoa beans globally and controlling the whole bean-to-bar process.

items.name: The Chocolate Line
items.description: Bruges chocolatier known for experimental flavours, run by Dominique Persoone and his son Julius.

items.name: Dumon
items.description: A Bruges chocolatier known for smooth, creamy pralines, located along Katelijnestraat's cluster of chocolate shops.

items.name: Delirium Café
items.description: Holds a Guinness World Record for beer selection (2,004 beers in 2004), including Belgian Trappist beers such as Rochefort 8/12 and Westmalle Tripel, in an 18th-century cellar near the Grand Place.
items.ticketInfo: No entry fee, pay per drink

items.name: Bruges day trip from Brussels
items.description: About 1 hour by direct train — canals, chocolate shops and the historic centre in a single day.

items.name: Leuven day trip from Brussels
items.description: Only ~25 minutes by train — the KU Leuven library, Oude Markt and botanical garden make for an easy half-day or full-day trip.

items.name: Mechelen day trip from Brussels or Antwerp
items.description: 20-25 minutes from either city — St. Rumbold's Tower and two UNESCO béguinages in a compact, walkable centre.

items.name: Durbuy
items.description: A tiny medieval town in the Ardennes, popularly promoted as 'the smallest city in the world', with cobblestone streets and artisanal shops.
items.ticketInfo: Free to explore; best reached by car

items.name: Kortrijk
items.description: A lesser-visited city blending medieval architecture (Broel Towers) with a contemporary arts scene on Buda Island.
